sourceMovie.title: G.I. Joe: Retaliation
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: G.I. Joe: Retaliation (2013) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Jon M. Chu's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Mission: Impossible - Ghost Protocol
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If G.I. Joe: Retaliation clicked for you, Mission: Impossible - Ghost Protocol (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after G.I. Joe: Retaliation?
faq.answer: Try Mission: Impossible - Ghost Protocol, The Expendables 3, Escape from New York—each shares DNA with G.I. Joe: Retaliation while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is G.I. Joe: Retaliation?
faq.answer: G.I. Joe: Retaliation (2013) is best described as Action, Science Fiction, Adventure, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is G.I. Joe: Retaliation part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "G.I. Joe (Live-Action) Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
